questions.question:
A graphics card has on board memory of 1 MB. Which of the following modes can the card not support?

(GATE CSE 2000)
options:
A.

1600 x 400 resolution with 256 colors on a 17 inch monitor

B.

1600 x 400 resolution with 16 million colors on a 14 inch monitor

C.

800 x 400 resolution with 16 million colors on a 17 inch monitor

D.

800 x 800 resolution with 256 colors on a 14 inch monitor
questions.correct_answer:
B.

1600 x 400 resolution with 16 million colors on a 14 inch monitor
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

Which of the following need not necessarily be saved on a context switch between processes?

(GATE CSE 2000)

questions.question:
Which of the following actions is/are typically not performed by the operating system when switching context from process A to process B?

(GATE CSE 1999)
options:
A.

Saving current register values and restoring saved register values for process B.

C.

Swapping out the memory image of process A to the disk.
questions.correct_answer:
C.

Swapping out the memory image of process A to the disk.
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Which of the following is/are advantage(s) of virtual memory?

(GATE CSE 1999)
options:
A.

Faster access to memory on an average.

B.

Processes can be given protected address spaces.

C.

Linker can assign addresses independent of where the program will be loaded in physical memory.

D.

Program larger than the physical memory size can be run.
questions.correct_answer:
B.

Processes can be given protected address spaces.
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Each Process \(P_i, i = 1\ldots 9\) is coded as follows

 repeat 
    P(mutex)
    {Critical section}
    V(mutex)
forever

The code for \(P_{10}\) is identical except it uses V(mutex) in place of P(mutex). What is the largest number of processes that can be inside the critical section at any moment?

(GATE CSE 1997)
